source: 2. UNSUPERVISED MACHINE TRANSLATION USING MONOLINGUAL CORPORA ONLY.pdf

concepts.finding: Fully unsupervised MT uses denoising autoencoders, shared encoder, and iterative back-translation to translate using only monolingual corpora, achieving 32.8 BLEU on Multi30k En-Fr without parallel data.
concepts.implication: Unsupervised cross-lingual transfer is possible with shared latent spaces and denoising, applicable to unsupervised query-to-document matching across languages.
concepts.open_question: Can unsupervised MT techniques enable cross-lingual search retrieval for low-resource language pairs?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: An unsupervised cross-lingual retrieval model trained only on monolingual corpora will achieve 70%+ of recall of supervised cross-lingual systems.
